params.description: Hundred Grid Mode B displays a 4 × 5 rectangle. 4 columns, 5 rows, 20 total tiles.
Mode C paired display shows both equation orientations. Left panel: 4 × 5 = 20. Right panel: 20 = 4 × 5. Equivalence bridge connects them.

params.stem: I built a 4-by-5 rectangle, so I can write ___ × ___ = ___ AND ___ = ___ × ___ — they say the same thing, and the equal sign means both sides have the same value.
params.description: Drop Down stem unlocks with six numeric slots. First blank set: 4, 5, 20. Second blank set: 20, 4, 5. Both equation orientations required.

beats.text: This rectangle lets you write the same fact in two ways. Finish the sentence using the numbers from both panels.
Fill in the six slots to write both equations.

validator.description: Student filled all six slots correctly: 4 × 5 = 20 AND 20 = 4 × 5

beats.text: You wrote both of them. 4 × 5 = 20 and 20 = 4 × 5 — the same fact, two ways. The equal sign means both sides have the same value.

elements.description: Hundred Grid Mode B displays a 4 × 5 rectangle. 4 columns, 5 rows, 20 total tiles.
Mode C paired display showing both equation orientations. Left panel: 4 × 5 = 20. Right panel: 20 = 4 × 5.
Completed fill-in-the-blank stem showing: I built a 4-by-5 rectangle, so I can write 4 × 5 = 20 AND 20 = 4 × 5 — they say the same thing, and the equal sign means both sides have the same value.
